div.p: How different attention heads learn different relationship types
These specializations are NOT programmed explicitly. During training, different heads naturally converge to different relationship types because:

ol.li: Different input slices: Each head sees different dimensions of the embedding (head 1: dims 0-63, head 2: dims 64-127, etc.)
Gradient pressure: When one head captures a useful pattern, it receives stronger gradients for that pattern, reinforcing specialization
Regularization effect: Splitting dimensions forces heads to specialize rather than all learning the same thing Empirical findings (from attention head analysis papers):

ul.li: ~30% of heads show clear syntactic patterns

div.p: Attends to sentence boundaries and commas
Learns which heads to trust for each context
